heroHeadline: Gunman Fires at White House Correspondents' Dinner; Trump Evacuated Unharmed — DC Circuit Strikes Down Asylum Ban
heroSubtitle: On April 25, gunman Cole Tomas Allen breached security at the Washington Hilton during the White House Correspondents' Dinner and opened fire — the first time Trump attended the event as president. Trump, VP Vance, and cabinet members were evacuated; one Secret Service officer was struck but protected by his vest. Acting AG Blanche said Allen was likely targeting Trump officials. On April 24, the DC Circuit ruled 2-1 that Trump's asylum suspension at the southern border is unlawful, dealing a major blow to his immigration agenda — with the administration vowing a Supreme Court appeal. Also on April 25, a federal judge ruled the Trump administration violated the First Amendment by pressuring Apple and Facebook to remove ICE-tracking apps.
footerNote: Data sourced from White House, Federal Register, executive orders, court records, Al Jazeera, CNN, Reuters, AP, NPR, Bloomberg, CNBC, Axios, NBC News, CBS News, Fox News, Gulf News, The National, Newsweek, Nate Silver/Silver Bulletin, YouGov/Economist, Fox News, BLS, Peterson Institute for International Economics, Tax Foundation, CFR, EIA, Treasury, Goldman Sachs, Federal Reserve, OECD, Cato Institute, Penn Wharton Budget Model, IMF.
